article_title: Nintendo Announces a Switch 2 Remake of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time, Due in 2026
summary: Accurate, neutral coverage of the June 9 Nintendo Direct Ocarina of Time Switch 2 remake reveal; every quote and specific verified verbatim against three snapshots. Sole finding is a non-blocking bare-hostname allowlist warning (nintendo.com), corroborated by two allowlisted outlets — no reader-facing factual error to correct.

content_preview.title: Nintendo Announces a Switch 2 Remake of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time, Due in 2026
content_preview.summary: Nintendo revealed a Switch 2 remake of the 1998 Nintendo 64 classic at its June 9 Direct, confirming a 2026 window but no studio, date, or gameplay.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nintendo has announced a remake of *The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time* for the Nintendo Switch 2, with the company describing it as a 2026 release available only on its latest console. The reveal came during a Nintendo Direct presentation, according to [Nintendo](https://www.nintendo.com/us/whatsnew/nintendo-direct-unveils-new-games-and-updates-for-nintendo-switch-2-and-nintendo-switch-including-the-legend-of-zelda-ocarina-of-time-kingdom-hearts-iv-xenoblade-genesis-and-more/), w...

recommendations: Consider adding the bare nintendo.com/whatsnew newsroom path to config/source_allowlist.txt so the official Nintendo press URL stops triggering a hostname warning.

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight, well-structured News piece (478 words, within the News 400-1200 range). Clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections. Appropriately restrained given how little Nintendo revealed; explicitly separates confirmed facts from open questions.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three source snapshots read from disk (status 200, no fallbacks). source-0.html.gz (Nintendo official whatsnew): confirms the 06/09/26 Direct, 'The Nintendo 64 classic returns for a new generation in 2026, reborn exclusively for Nintendo Switch 2', Kingdom Hearts IV 'will be available at launch on Nintendo Switch 2', and Xenoblade Genesis 'coming to Nintendo Switch 2 in 2027' — all verbatim. source-1.html.gz (Video Games Chronicle, Chris Scullion, 9th Jun 2026): confirms the same 'reborn exclusively' quote, the Metacritic score of 99 'topping the site's list of the best games of all time' (single-source attribution, no compound citation), 1998 N64 original, 2011 3DS remaster with improved controls/touchscreen, the 'It's not yet clear which studio is working on the Ocarina of Time remake' quote, the teaser trailer, and 'Nintendo also released a single image for the game.' source-2.html.gz (Nintendo Everything, June 9 2026): confirms the remake announcement, 'launching in 2026', the same blurb, 'Nintendo only debuted a short trailer with little shown' (verbatim), and the 2011 3DS remaster.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific traces to a cited source. Metacritic 99 is attributed to VGC alone (no compound citation). Kingdom Hearts IV / Xenoblade Genesis specifics are cited to Nintendo's official page and match it verbatim. The article's 'a teaser trailer and a single screenshot' is a faithful paraphrase of VGC's 'A teaser trailer ... Nintendo also released a single image for the game' (image -> screenshot). No hallucinated quotes detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. The Analysis section stays grounded ('the practical shape of the remake will depend on details Nintendo has so far withheld') rather than speculating.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of an Ocarina of Time remake or this Direct. Not a duplicate.
concerns: Non-blocking: the Nintendo source is cited via the bare hostname nintendo.com, which is not on the allowlist (allowlist lists nintendo.com/whatsnew). The headline and lead are independently corroborated by the two allowlisted outlets (VGC, Nintendo Everything), so this does not affect verifiability.

The article correctly EXCLUDED the Zelda 'series turned 40 / 40th anniversary' framing. That detail does appear in the VGC and Nintendo Everything bodies (and in VGC as Nintendo's president declining to comment on anniversary plans), but it is tangential to the remake news and its omission keeps the piece focused. No fabrication.
recommendations: Optionally add the official Nintendo newsroom path to the allowlist to clear the hostname warning on future Nintendo press-release citations.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate, neutral submission. All quotes verbatim, all specifics sourced, headline/summary/lead fully backed, no compound citation, no duplicate. The sole automated finding is a non-blocking allowlist hostname warning that is not a reader-facing factual error, so no corrections record is warranted. APPROVE.
